tier_2_handbook.definition: Breast cancer is a malignant neoplasm arising from the epithelial cells of the breast, encompassing a spectrum of diseases ranging from noninvasive ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) to invasive carcinomas with potential for metastasis. The NCCN Guidelines define breast cancer as the most common malignancy in females in the United States, with an estimated 313,510 new cases and 42,780 deaths in 2024 [1,2]. DCIS is characterized by proliferation of malignant epithelial cells confined to the mammary ducts without invasion through the basement membrane, staged as Tis,N0,M0 [DCIS-1]. Invasive breast cancer includes carcinoma of no special type (NST) and special histologic subtypes such as lobular, tubular, mucinous, and metaplastic carcinomas [BINV-4]. The primary tumor arises from the terminal duct-lobular unit, with embryological origin from ectodermal breast bud invaginating into mesenchyme. Anatomically, the breast extends from the clavicle to the inframammary fold and from the sternum to the midaxillary line, with lymphatic drainage primarily to axillary, internal mammary, and supraclavicular nodes [BINV-E]. Inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) is a distinct clinical entity defined by rapid onset of erythema, edema, and peau d'orange involving at least one-third of the breast, classified as T4d [IBC-1]. The AJCC TNM staging system categorizes disease based on tumor size, nodal involvement, and distant metastasis, with addition of biomarker status (ER, PR, HER2) and grade for prognostic staging [ST-1 to ST-11].

subtypes.name: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS)
subtypes.description: Noninvasive proliferation of malignant epithelial cells within the mammary ducts without stromal invasion. Subclassified by nuclear grade (low, intermediate, high) and architectural pattern (e.g., comedo, cribriform, solid). Encapsulated papillary carcinoma without invasion is staged as pTis due to behavior similar to DCIS [DCIS-1].
subtypes.frequency: Over 50,000 cases diagnosed annually in the United States (2022 estimate) [MS-3]. Accounts for approximately 20-25% of all breast cancer diagnoses.
subtypes.key_associations: ER positivity (tested to determine benefit of endocrine therapy for risk reduction [DCIS-1]); HER2 status not routinely recommended for DCIS [MS-3]; risk factors include larger size, palpable mass, grade III, margins ≤2 mm, ER-negative tumors, age <50 [DCIS-1].

subtypes.name: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (NST)
subtypes.description: Most common invasive breast cancer type, encompassing multiple patterns including medullary pattern, cancers with neuroendocrine expression, and other rare patterns [BINV-4]. Represents the default category when no special histologic pattern predominates.
subtypes.key_associations: Variable ER/PR and HER2 expression; gene expression profiling identifies luminal A, luminal B, HER2-enriched, and basal-like subtypes.

subtypes.name: Invasive lobular carcinoma
subtypes.description: Characterized by discobesive cells that infiltrate stroma in a linear, single-file pattern. Often multifocal and bilateral. May be difficult to detect on mammography [BINV-1 footnote b, BINV-4].
subtypes.key_associations: High rate of ER positivity (80-95%); HER2 negativity common; associated with loss of E-cadherin expression; caution with omission of SLNB due to lobular histology [BINV-1 footnote b].

subtypes.name: Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC)
subtypes.description: Invasive breast cancer lacking expression of ER, PR, and HER2 (IHC 0 or 1+ or 2+/ISH negative). Associated with aggressive behavior and high risk of recurrence [BINV-10, BINV-Q 3 of 15].
subtypes.frequency: Approximately 15-20% of invasive breast cancers; more common in younger women and African American women.
subtypes.key_associations: Basal-like subtype on gene expression; germline BRCA1/2 mutations in about 10-20% of TNBC; PD-L1 expression (CPS ≥10) predicts benefit from pembrolizumab [BINV-Q 3 of 15]; high genomic instability.

subtypes.name: HER2-positive breast cancer
subtypes.description: Invasive breast cancer with overexpression/amplification of HER2 (ERBB2) defined as IHC 3+ or ISH ratio ≥2.0 with average HER2 copy number ≥4.0 signals/cell [BINV-A 2-3 of 4]. Includes both HR-positive and HR-negative subtypes.
subtypes.key_associations: High grade; aggressive but highly responsive to HER2-targeted therapy (trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DM1, fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan); increased risk of CNS metastases.

subtypes.name: Favorable histologic types
subtypes.description: Includes pure tubular (≥90% tubular structures), pure mucinous (extracellular mucin >90%), pure cribriform, adenoid cystic carcinoma, secretory carcinoma, low-grade adenosquamous carcinoma, and other rare low-grade forms. Must be pure (>90% on surgical excision) and HER2-negative to be considered favorable [BINV-11].
subtypes.frequency: Tubular 1-2%, mucinous 1-6%, cribriform rare, adenoid cystic <1% of breast cancers.
subtypes.key_associations: Overwhelmingly ER-positive (especially tubular, mucinous, cribriform); hormone receptor positivity; low risk of recurrence; endocrine therapy recommended for most; chemotherapy rarely indicated [BINV-11].

subtypes.name: Inflammatory breast cancer (IBC)
subtypes.description: Clinical diagnosis requiring erythema, edema (peau d'orange) of at least one-third of the breast, with or without a palpable mass, and pathologic confirmation of invasive carcinoma. Dermal lymphatic invasion may be present but not required [IBC-1].
subtypes.frequency: Estimated 1-6% of breast cancer cases in the United States [MS-93].
subtypes.key_associations: Usually HR-negative, more frequently HER2-positive compared to non-inflammatory breast cancers [MS-93]; presentation at younger age; high risk of local and distant recurrence; requires multimodal treatment (chemotherapy, mastectomy, radiation).

subtypes.name: Paget disease of the breast
subtypes.description: Rare manifestation characterized by neoplastic Paget cells in the epidermis of the nipple-areolar complex, presenting with eczema, ulceration, bleeding, or itching. Associated with underlying DCIS or invasive carcinoma in up to 80-90% of cases [PAGET-1, MS-88].
subtypes.key_associations: Associated with underlying breast malignancy; central lumpectomy including NAC ± SLNB with whole breast RT or total mastectomy including NAC [PAGET-2]; axillary staging per invasive cancer guidelines if underlying invasive disease.

subtypes.name: Phyllodes tumor
subtypes.description: Rare fibroepithelial tumor composed of stromal and epithelial elements, classified as benign, borderline, or malignant [PHYLL-1]. Can be difficult to distinguish from fibroadenoma on core biopsy.
subtypes.frequency: Less than 1% of all breast tumors.
subtypes.key_associations: Mean age at diagnosis 40 years; rapid growth; Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53 mutation) increases risk [MS-90]; treatment is wide local excision with ≥1 cm margins; axillary staging not indicated; endocrine therapy and chemotherapy have no proven role [MS-90].

tier_2_handbook.molecular_pathogenesis: Breast cancer arises through accumulation of genomic alterations that drive malignant transformation and progression. Key driver mutations include activating mutations in PIK3CA, which occur in approximately 40% of HR-positive, HER2-negative breast cancers and predict benefit from PI3Kα inhibitors (alpelisib, inavolisib) [MS-65; BINV-Q 6 of 15]. The PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling pathway is frequently activated, with AKT1 and PTEN alterations also targetable by capivasertib [MS-65; BINV-Q 6 of 15]. ESR1 mutations, particularly in the ligand-binding domain, emerge under selective pressure of aromatase inhibitor therapy and are detected by ctDNA in approximately 30-40% of AI-resistant, HR-positive metastatic breast cancers; these are targetable by selective estrogen receptor degraders (elacestrant, imlunestrant, vepdegestrant) [MS-67-68; BINV-Q 6 of 15]. HER2 (ERBB2) amplification occurs in ~15-20% of breast cancers and defines a subtype driven by constitutive HER2 signaling, conferring sensitivity to trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DM1, and fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan [BINV-A 2-3 of 4]. Approximately 5% of breast cancers harbor germline BRCA1 or BRCA2 pathogenic variants, which cause defective homologous recombination repair and sensitivity to PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) [MS-83; BINV-Q 7 of 15]. Additional germline mutations in PALB2, ATM, CHEK2, and TP53 contribute to hereditary breast cancer risk [MS-84; BINV-1 footnote f]. Somatic mutations in BRCA1/2 and other homologous recombination genes occur in a subset of TNBC [MS-85]. NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions are rare but targetable with larotrectinib, entrectinib, and repotrectinib [MS-84; BINV-Q 7 of 15]. RET gene fusions are targetable with selpercatinib [MS-85; BINV-Q 7 of 15]. Microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H)/mismatch repair deficiency (dMMR) and high tumor mutational burden (TMB-H ≥10 mut/Mb) are rare in breast cancer but confer sensitivity to PD-1 blockade (pembrolizumab) [MS-84-85; BINV-Q 7 of 15]. Chromosomal abnormalities include amplification of MYC, CCND1, and FGFR1, though these are not currently therapeutically targetable with FDA-approved drugs in breast cancer. The intrinsic molecular subtypes (luminal A, luminal B, HER2-enriched, basal-like) identified by gene expression profiling correlate with prognosis and treatment response, with basal-like (largely TNBC) showing high proliferation and genomic instability [MS-41; BINV-N 3 of 5].
tier_2_handbook.epidemiology: Breast cancer is the most common malignancy among women in the United States, with an estimated 313,510 new invasive cases and 42,780 deaths projected for 2024 [1,2]. The American Cancer Society reported that in 2022, over 50,000 cases of ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) were diagnosed [MS-3]. Incidence rates have been relatively stable, with a slight increase possibly due to screening and obesity trends. Breast cancer is the second leading cause of cancer death in women, after lung cancer [1]. Inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) accounts for 1-6% of breast cancer cases in the US [MS-93]. Approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients carry a germline BRCA1 or BRCA2 pathogenic variant, with higher prevalence among those with triple-negative disease [MS-83]. The median age at diagnosis is 62 years, and risk increases with age. Race/ethnicity disparities exist: Black women have a lower incidence than White women but a higher mortality rate (age-adjusted death rate approximately 40% higher) [1]. The lifetime risk of developing breast cancer in US women is about 13% (1 in 8). Five-year relative survival for localized breast cancer exceeds 99%, but for distant-stage disease it is approximately 31% [1]. DCIS incidence has increased since the introduction of screening mammography [MS-3]. Phyllodes tumors occur at a mean age of 40 years, younger than invasive ductal carcinoma [MS-90]. Pregnancy-associated breast cancer is infrequent, with 1.3 cases per 10,000 live births in a California registry study [MS-91]. Male breast cancer accounts for less than 1% of all breast cancers, and management recommendations are extrapolated from female breast cancer studies [BINV-J 1 of 2].

risk_factors.detail: Incidence increases with age; median age at diagnosis 62 years. Young age (<50 years) is a significant predictor of increased likelihood of ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence after breast-conserving therapy [MS-13].

Approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients carry germline BRCA1/2 mutations. Higher prevalence in triple-negative breast cancer (10-20%). Associated with high risk of breast and ovarian cancer [MS-83]. Genetic testing recommended for all patients with TNBC, those at risk per NCCN genetic criteria, and those being considered for adjuvant olaparib [BINV-1].
risk_factors.strength: Strong; relative risk for breast cancer up to 10-30 fold depending on population.

risk_factors.factor: Other germline pathogenic variants (TP53, ATM, PALB2, CHEK2, CDH1, PTEN, STK11)
risk_factors.detail: Rare mutations in TP53 (Li-Fraumeni syndrome) increase risk of phyllodes tumors and breast cancer [MS-90]. PALB2 mutations associated with homologous recombination deficiency and benefit from olaparib [MS-84]. ATM, CHEK2, CDH1, PTEN, and STK11 are established breast cancer susceptibility genes per NCCN genetic risk assessment guidelines [BINV-1 footnote f].
risk_factors.strength: Moderate to strong; PALB2 confers 3-5 fold increased risk; TP53 associated with very high risk but rare.

risk_factors.factor: Family history of breast or ovarian cancer
risk_factors.detail: Family history in a first-degree relative (mother, sister, daughter) increases risk. The NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment define criteria for genetic counseling based on family history patterns [BINV-1].
risk_factors.strength: Moderate; risk increases with number of affected relatives and younger age at diagnosis.

risk_factors.detail: Obesity associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in a nested case-control study of 369 patients with ER-positive tumors [MS-53]. Also associated with poorer outcomes for certain breast cancer subtypes.

Smoking associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in a nested case-control study [MS-53]. Smoking also increases complications in breast reconstruction (wound healing, necrosis, infection) [BINV-H 6 of 7].
risk_factors.strength: Moderate for breast cancer risk; strong for surgical complications.

risk_factors.detail: Alcohol consumption associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer [MS-53]. The NCCN Guidelines recommend limited alcohol intake for optimal breast cancer outcomes [BINV-17].

Dense breasts are a risk factor for breast cancer and can mask tumors on mammography. MRI screening recommended for patients with dense breasts and history of breast cancer [BINV-B]. Annual MRI recommended for patients with prior breast cancer diagnosed at age ≤50 or with dense breasts [BINV-B].
risk_factors.strength: Moderate; relative risk 4-6 fold for extremely dense vs. fatty breasts.

risk_factors.detail: History of prior RT to the affected area increases risk for subsequent breast cancer. Use of RT for treatment of Hodgkin lymphoma is a known risk factor. The NCCN Guidelines list prior RT as a relative contraindication to breast-conserving approaches requiring RT [BINV-G]. Knowledge of prior doses and volumes is important [BINV-G].
risk_factors.strength: Strong; especially for exposure at young age.

risk_factors.detail: Menopausal hormone therapy is relatively contraindicated in survivors of hormonally mediated cancers; should be used with caution in those with increased genetic cancer risk [BINV-17]. The NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship provide further detail.
risk_factors.strength: Moderate; evidence from WHI and other observational studies.

protective_factors: Active lifestyle and regular physical activity: Evidence suggests that an active lifestyle, healthy diet, limited alcohol intake, and achieving and maintaining an ideal body weight (BMI 20-25) may lead to optimal breast cancer outcomes [BINV-17; MS-54]. A prospective study of 1490 patients with stage I–III breast cancer showed an association between high fruit and vegetable consumption, physical activity, and improved survivorship [MS-53]. The NCCN Panel recommends an active lifestyle and ideal body weight for optimal overall health and breast cancer outcomes [MS-54].

Tamoxifen for risk reduction: In patients with ER-positive DCIS treated with breast-conserving therapy, tamoxifen reduces risk of ipsilateral breast cancer recurrence (category 1 for those undergoing BCT followed by RT; category 2A for excision alone) [DCIS-2]. The NSABP B-24 trial showed a 3.4% absolute reduction in ipsilateral in-breast tumor recurrence risk at 13.6 years follow-up (HR 0.30; 95% CI 0.21–0.42) [MS-8]. The Breast Cancer Prevention Trial (NSABP P-1) showed a 75% reduction in occurrence of invasive breast cancer in patients with atypical ductal hyperplasia treated with tamoxifen [MS-8]. Low-dose tamoxifen (5 mg/day for 3 years) reduces intraepithelial neoplasia recurrence (HR 0.48; 95% CI 0.25–0.89) [MS-8].

Aromatase inhibitor therapy for risk reduction: For postmenopausal patients with ER-positive DCIS treated by wide local excision ± RT, anastrozole provides comparable benefit to tamoxifen with a different toxicity profile (IBIS-II and NSABP B-35 trials) [MS-8-9]. Anastrozole resulted in a statistically significant decrease in breast cancer-free interval events compared with tamoxifen (HR 0.73; 95% CI 0.56–0.96) in the NSABP B-35 study [MS-9].

Breastfeeding: Breastfeeding after breast-conservation therapy for breast cancer is not contraindicated, and may offer protective effects for the contralateral breast, though the guideline does not explicitly state a risk reduction effect [BINV-C]. The NCCN Panel notes that breastfeeding during active treatment with chemotherapy and endocrine therapy is not recommended [BINV-C].

Bisphosphonate therapy for bone health and potential risk reduction: The use of bisphosphonates (oral/IV) is acceptable to maintain or improve bone mineral density and reduce risk of fractures in postmenopausal patients receiving adjuvant aromatase inhibitor therapy [BINV-K 1 of 4]. A meta-analysis by the Early Breast Cancer Trialists' Collaborative Group showed adjuvant bisphosphonates reduce bone recurrence (RR 0.83) and breast cancer mortality in postmenopausal patients [MS-45].

Ovarian suppression with GnRH agonists for fertility preservation: Randomized trials showed that GnRH agonists administered during adjuvant chemotherapy protect against ovarian failure and reduce the risk of early menopause, and improve the probability of pregnancy from 11% to 21% in patients with HR-negative early-stage breast cancer [MS-12]. Smaller experiences in HR-positive disease show conflicting results [MS-12].

clinical_features.typical_presentation: Breast cancer encompasses a spectrum of diseases ranging from non-invasive ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) to invasive carcinomas with distinct biological subtypes. DCIS is typically asymptomatic and detected on screening mammography as microcalcifications; symptoms such as a palpable mass, nipple discharge, or Paget disease may occur [DCIS-1]. Invasive breast cancer most commonly presents as a painless, firm breast mass or thickening, often discovered by the patient or on imaging. Other presentations include skin dimpling (peau d'orange), nipple retraction, erythema, or ulceration. Inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) is characterized by rapid onset (≤6 months) of erythema, edema, peau d'orange occupying ≥1/3 of the breast, with or without a palpable mass [IBC-1]. Paget disease presents with eczematous changes, ulceration, bleeding, or itching of the nipple-areolar complex [PAGET-1]. Phyllodes tumors present as a rapidly growing, painless, large (often >3 cm) mass, with imaging suggestive of fibroadenoma but with rapid growth [PHYLL-1]. Breast cancer in males typically presents as a painless retroareolar mass; due to rarity, diagnosis may be delayed. Pregnancy-associated breast cancer often presents with a mass, but diagnosis may be delayed due to physiologic breast changes [PREG-1].

symptoms.name: breast mass or thickening
symptoms.detail: Most common symptom of invasive breast cancer; patients often detect a painless, firm lump.
symptoms.alarm: any new, persistent mass requires evaluation

symptoms.name: nipple discharge
symptoms.detail: Can be serous, bloody, or watery; unilateral, spontaneous discharge is more concerning.
symptoms.alarm: bloody discharge or associated with mass requires workup

symptoms.name: skin changes
symptoms.detail: Erythema, edema, peau d'orange, dimpling, or ulceration; characteristic of IBC and advanced tumors.
symptoms.alarm: rapid onset of skin changes suggests IBC

symptoms.name: Paget disease of nipple
symptoms.detail: Eczema, ulceration, bleeding, or itching of nipple-areola; often associated with underlying carcinoma.
symptoms.alarm: persistent nipple eczematous changes require biopsy

symptoms.name: axillary mass
symptoms.detail: Enlarged lymph nodes may be first sign; can represent nodal metastasis from occult primary.
symptoms.frequency: ~30% of patients present with palpable nodes
symptoms.alarm: fixed or matted nodes indicate advanced disease

signs.name: palpable breast mass
signs.detail: Firm, irregular, poorly mobile mass; may be tender in inflammatory cases.
signs.frequency: Most common sign of invasive cancer

signs.name: skin edema/peau d'orange
signs.detail: Dermal lymphatic involvement causing skin thickening and pitting; hallmark of IBC.

signs.name: nipple retraction or flattening
signs.detail: Due to tumor involvement of lactiferous ducts or fibrosis.
signs.frequency: Variable; more common in central tumors

signs.name: axillary lymphadenopathy
signs.detail: Palpable, firm, sometimes fixed nodes; may be the only sign in occult breast cancer.
signs.frequency: Up to 50% of patients at diagnosis

signs.name: inflammatory changes
signs.detail: Erythema, warmth, and edema; often mistaken for mastitis; requires urgent differentiation.

atypical_or_missed: Inflammatory breast cancer can be misdiagnosed as mastitis or cellulitis, delaying oncologic treatment [IBC-1].
Paget disease is often mistaken for dermatitis or eczema, leading to months of topical treatment before diagnosis [PAGET-1].
Male breast cancer may be overlooked because breast cancer is rare in males; diagnosis often occurs at later stage [BINV-J].
Breast cancer during pregnancy may be masked by physiologic breast hypertrophy, leading to diagnostic delay [PREG-1].
Phyllodes tumors can be mistaken for fibroadenomas due to similar imaging features; rapid growth should raise suspicion [PHYLL-1].

red_flags: Rapid onset of breast erythema, edema, and peau d'orange occupying ≥1/3 of the breast – suspicious for inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) and requires urgent biopsy and imaging [IBC-1].
Persistent nipple eczema, ulceration, bleeding, or itching – Paget disease; biopsy of nipple-areola complex is indicated [PAGET-1].
Rapid enlargement of a breast mass over weeks to months – may indicate phyllodes tumor or high-grade invasive cancer [PHYLL-1].
Fixed, matted axillary lymph nodes, supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, or skin ulceration – suggests advanced locoregional disease and requires systemic staging [BINV-1].
New neurological symptoms, back pain, or unexplained hypercalcemia – may indicate metastatic disease to CNS, spine, or bone; urgent imaging needed [BINV-18].
Weight loss, persistent cough, or abdominal pain – could indicate visceral metastases; perform CT imaging [BINV-18].
Bleeding from the nipple or skin breakdown over tumor – requires urgent surgical evaluation.
Inability to achieve negative margins after multiple attempts at breast-conserving surgery – may require mastectomy [BINV-F].
Pregnancy with breast cancer and need for RT or chemotherapy – requires coordination with obstetrics and consideration of timing to avoid harm to fetus [PREG-1].
Presence of germline BRCA1/2, PALB2, or TP53 mutation – increases risk for contralateral breast cancer and may influence surgical decisions [BINV-1].

diagnostic.rationale: Primary imaging modality for detection and characterization of breast lesions; identifies microcalcifications, masses, and architectural distortion.
diagnostic.key_findings: Spiculated mass, pleomorphic microcalcifications, suspicious asymmetry [DCIS-1, BINV-1].

diagnostic.rationale: Used to characterize lesions identified on mammogram, assess cystic/solid nature, and guide biopsy; also for axillary evaluation.
diagnostic.key_findings: Hypoechoic mass with irregular margins, posterior shadowing; suspicious lymph nodes with cortical thickening [BINV-1].

diagnostic.rationale: Tissue diagnosis; prefer core over FNA for histologic subtype, grade, and biomarker assessment.
diagnostic.key_findings: Invasive carcinoma or DCIS; permits ER/PR/HER2 testing [BINV-1, BINV-A].

diagnostic.rationale: Diagnosis of Paget disease; identifies Paget cells in epidermis.
diagnostic.key_findings: Large, pale Paget cells with prominent nucleoli [PAGET-1].

diagnostic.rationale: Optional for further characterization of extent of disease, especially in dense breasts, mammographically occult tumors, or for evaluating axillary metastasis with occult primary.
diagnostic.key_findings: Enhancing mass or non-mass enhancement with washout kinetics; can overestimate disease extent; no proven improvement in re-excision rates or survival [BINV-B].

diagnostic.test: Axillary ultrasound with FNA or core biopsy
diagnostic.rationale: Evaluates nodal status if suspicious for metastasis; guides surgical planning.
diagnostic.key_findings: Cortical thickening >3 mm, loss of fatty hilum; positive cytology for malignant cells [BINV-D].

staging.rationale: Indicated for patients with signs/symptoms of metastatic disease or clinically high-risk (T3-4, N2-3); evaluates for lung/pleural metastases.
staging.indication: Presence of pulmonary symptoms, elevated alkaline phosphatase, or high-risk features [BINV-1, BINV-12].

staging.test: Abdomen ± pelvis diagnostic CT with contrast or MRI with contrast
staging.rationale: Assesses for liver, adrenal, and peritoneal metastases.
staging.indication: Abnormal LFTs, abdominal symptoms, or high-risk disease [BINV-1, BINV-12].

staging.test: Bone scan or sodium fluoride PET/CT
staging.rationale: Detects osteoblastic bone metastases; sodium fluoride PET/CT more sensitive but category 2B.
staging.indication: Bone pain, elevated alkaline phosphatase, or high-risk disease (especially T3-4, N2-3, or IBC) [BINV-1, BINV-12, IBC-1].

staging.rationale: Useful in advanced disease (stage III) for detection of unsuspected nodal and distant metastases; can be performed with diagnostic CT.
staging.indication: Optional for standard staging; most beneficial for advanced disease and invasive ductal histology; may be used when standard studies equivocal [BINV-12].

staging.rationale: Evaluates for CNS metastases in patients with neurological symptoms.
staging.indication: Suspicious CNS symptoms (headache, seizures, focal deficits) [BINV-18].

staging.rationale: Assesses for spinal cord compression in patients with back pain or neurological symptoms.
staging.indication: Back pain suggestive of cord compression [BINV-18].

biomarkers.rationale: Determines eligibility for endocrine therapy; 1–100% staining considered ER-positive; 1–10% is ER-low-positive.
biomarkers.when: All new primary and newly metastatic breast cancers; also for DCIS (for risk reduction).

biomarkers.rationale: Aids in prognosis and serves as control for ER; positive if ≥1% nuclear staining.
biomarkers.when: All invasive primary and metastatic samples.

biomarkers.test: HER2 (ERBB2) testing by IHC and/or ISH
biomarkers.rationale: Determines eligibility for HER2-targeted therapies; ASCO/CAP guidelines define positivity (IHC 3+ or ISH amplified).
biomarkers.when: All new primary and metastatic breast cancers.

biomarkers.rationale: Identifies candidates for targeted therapies (e.g., PARP inhibitors for germline BRCA1/2, PIK3CA inhibitors, NTRK fusions).
biomarkers.when: All patients with recurrent or metastatic disease; consider for early-stage high-risk disease (e.g., TNBC, young age, family history).
biomarkers.mandatory: Recommended for recurrent/stage IV disease; for early-stage, consider based on risk.

biomarkers.rationale: Determines eligibility for pembrolizumab in advanced TNBC (first-line if CPS ≥10).
biomarkers.when: In patients with unresectable locally advanced or metastatic TNBC.

biomarkers.rationale: Identifies resistance to aromatase inhibitors and eligibility for elacestrant, imlunestrant, or other SERDs.
biomarkers.when: At progression after prior lines of endocrine therapy in metastatic HR+/HER2- breast cancer.

biomarkers.rationale: Determines eligibility for alpelisib, capivasertib, or inavolisib in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer.
biomarkers.when: At progression after endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor.
biomarkers.mandatory: Recommended if considering these targeted therapies.

biomarkers.rationale: Identifies candidates for PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) and influences surgical decisions.
biomarkers.when: All patients with recurrent/metastatic breast cancer; consider for all patients with TNBC at any age or those at risk for hereditary syndrome.
biomarkers.mandatory: Yes for recurrent/metastatic; category 1 for germline BRCA1/2.

biomarkers.rationale: May aid in prognostication and treatment decisions, particularly for HR+/HER2- disease when considering adjuvant abemaciclib.
biomarkers.when: Consider for HR+/HER2- early breast cancer if considering adjuvant CDK4/6 inhibitor.

biomarkers.test: 21-gene recurrence score (Oncotype DX) or other genomic assays
biomarkers.rationale: Provides prognostic and predictive information for chemotherapy benefit in HR+/HER2- early breast cancer.
biomarkers.when: Strongly consider for node-negative or 1–3 positive nodes (postmenopausal) HR+/HER2- tumors to guide adjuvant chemotherapy decisions.
biomarkers.mandatory: Category 1 for node-negative; category 2A for node-positive.

do_not_miss: HER2 testing by validated IHC/ISH per ASCO/CAP guidelines on all primary and metastatic specimens [BINV-A].
ER/PR testing on all invasive cancers and DCIS [BINV-A].
Multigene panel testing (somatic and germline) for all patients with recurrent or stage IV disease [BINV-18].
Assess for germline BRCA1/2 PVs in all recurrent/metastatic breast cancer and in patients with TNBC at any age [BINV-18].
For patients with bilateral or suspicious axillary nodes, perform axillary ultrasound with FNA/core biopsy before definitive surgery [BINV-D].
Consider brain MRI in patients with CNS symptoms or high-risk subtypes (HER2+, TNBC) at first recurrence [BINV-18].
avoid: Routine systemic staging (chest/abdomen/pelvis CT, bone scan, PET/CT) in asymptomatic patients with early-stage breast cancer (stages I–II) without signs/symptoms of metastases [BINV-1].

Routine use of breast MRI for all patients; available data do not demonstrate improvement in re-excision rates, local recurrence, or survival for DCIS or invasive cancer patients pursuing BCS [BINV-B].
Routine cytokeratin immunohistochemistry to define sentinel node involvement; treatment decisions should be based on H&E staining alone [BINV-D].
Routine use of tumor markers (CEA, CA 15-3, CA 27.29) for screening or monitoring in the absence of clinical suspicion [BINV-17, BINV-R].
Routine annual pelvic ultrasound for patients on tamoxifen; only age-appropriate gynecologic screening is recommended [BINV-17].
Use of hormone-based birth control in breast cancer survivors regardless of HR status [BINV-C].
Menopausal hormone therapy in survivors of hormonally mediated cancers; use with caution if increased genetic risk [BINV-17].
Performance of sentinel lymph node biopsy for pure DCIS unless there is concern for invasion, persistently positive margins, or mastectomy in patients >50 years with low-risk DCIS [DCIS-1].

staging.system: AJCC 8th edition, TNM staging for breast cancer (Anatomic Stage Groups and Prognostic Stage Groups) [ST-1 to ST-11]

t_stages.description: Paget disease of the nipple NOT associated with invasive carcinoma and/or DCIS in the underlying parenchyma.
Tumor >1 mm but ≤5 mm (round any measurement >1.0–1.9 mm to 2 mm).
Extension to chest wall (not including pectoralis muscle alone).
Ulceration and/or ipsilateral macroscopic satellite nodules and/or edema (peau d'orange) of skin not meeting inflammatory carcinoma criteria.
Inflammatory carcinoma (clinical diagnosis of erythema and edema involving ≥1/3 of the breast).

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed (e.g., previously removed).
No regional lymph node metastases by imaging or clinical examination.
Metastases to movable ipsilateral level I, II axillary lymph node(s). (Also includes cN1mi: micrometastases >0.2 mm but ≤2.0 mm.)
Metastases in ipsilateral level I, II axillary nodes fixed or matted to each other or to other structures.
Metastases only in ipsilateral internal mammary nodes in the absence of axillary node metastases.
Metastases in ipsilateral infraclavicular (level III axillary) node(s).
Metastases in ipsilateral internal mammary node(s) and axillary node(s).
Metastases in ipsilateral supraclavicular node(s).
Regional nodes cannot be assessed (e.g., not removed or previously removed).
No regional lymph node metastasis identified or ITCs only.
ITCs only (malignant cell clusters ≤0.2 mm).
Positive molecular findings by RT-PCR; no ITCs detected.
Micrometastases (>0.2 mm but ≤2.0 mm).
Metastases in 1–3 axillary lymph nodes, at least one >2.0 mm.
Metastases in ipsilateral internal mammary sentinel nodes (excluding ITCs).
Combined pN1a and pN1b.
Metastases in 4–9 axillary lymph nodes (at least one >2.0 mm).
Metastases in clinically detected internal mammary nodes with pathologically negative axillary nodes.
Metastases in ≥10 axillary nodes, or metastases to infraclavicular (level III) nodes.
pN1a or pN2a in the presence of cN2b (positive internal mammary nodes by imaging); or pN2a in the presence of pN1b.
Metastases in ipsilateral supraclavicular nodes.

m_stages.description: No clinical or radiographic evidence of distant metastases.

No clinical or radiographic evidence of distant metastases but tumor cells or deposits ≤0.2 mm detected by microscopy or molecular techniques in circulating blood, bone marrow, or nonregional nodal tissue without symptoms or signs of metastases.
Distant metastases detected by clinical and radiographic means.
Any histologically proven metastases in distant organs; or nonregional nodes with metastases >0.2 mm.

stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Non-invasive cancer (DCIS or Paget without invasion).
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative – local therapy (surgery ± RT, endocrine therapy if ER+ DCIS)

stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Small invasive tumor (≤2 cm) with no nodal involvement.
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative – surgery ± RT, endocrine therapy if HR+, consider chemotherapy based on risk

stage_groups.criteria: T0 N1mi M0 or T1 N1mi M0
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative – similar to stage IA with consideration of axillary management

stage_groups.criteria: T0 N1 M0; T1 N1 M0; T2 N0 M0
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Node-positive (1–3 nodes) with small tumor or larger node-negative tumor.
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative – surgery ± RT, systemic therapy based on biomarkers

stage_groups.criteria: T2 N1 M0; T3 N0 M0
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative – surgery, systemic therapy, RT as indicated

stage_groups.criteria: T0 N2 M0; T1 N2 M0; T2 N2 M0; T3 N1 M0; T3 N2 M0
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Locally advanced – extensive nodal involvement (4–9 nodes) or larger primary with nodal spread.
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative with multimodal therapy (chemotherapy, surgery, RT)

stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Chest wall/skin involvement (including inflammatory breast cancer) with or without regional nodal disease.
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative with multimodal therapy (systemic therapy, surgery, RT); IBC requires neoadjuvant chemotherapy

stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Regional nodal involvement including supraclavicular or internal mammary nodes.

stage_groups.treatment_intent: Palliative – systemic therapy ± local therapy for symptom control; consider locoregional treatment for de novo oligometastatic disease with favorable features [BINV-18]

staging_pearls: The AJCC 8th edition includes both Anatomic Stage Groups and Prognostic Stage Groups (Clinical and Pathological), which incorporate grade, HER2, ER, and PR status [ST-6–ST-11].
For patients treated with preoperative systemic therapy, stage designation uses 'yc' or 'yp' prefixes; no anatomic stage group is assigned if pCR is achieved (e.g., ypT0ypN0cM0) [ST-3].
Inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) is staged as T4d regardless of tumor size; requires clinical diagnosis of erythema and edema occupying ≥1/3 of breast [IBC-1, ST-1].
Lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) is considered a benign entity and removed from AJCC staging (8th ed.) [ST-1].
Micrometastases (N1mi) are staged as IB if T0 or T1; for T2–4, they are included in the N1 category [ST-3].
Clinical staging should be used for patients receiving preoperative systemic therapy; final staging is based on pathologic findings after surgery.
The Prognostic Stage Groups are based on populations treated with appropriate endocrine and/or systemic chemotherapy (including anti-HER2 therapy) [ST-8].
Oncotype DX recurrence score <11 in T1–2N0 HER2-negative ER-positive cancer assigns Pathological Prognostic Stage IA regardless of grade [ST-11].

management_principles.overview: Breast cancer management requires a multimodal, multidisciplinary approach integrating surgery, radiation therapy, systemic therapy (chemotherapy, endocrine therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy), and supportive care. Treatment selection is driven by tumor biology (ER/PR, HER2 status, Ki-67, genomic assays), anatomic stage (TNM), patient menopausal status, genetic predisposition (e.g., BRCA1/2), and performance status. The NCCN Guidelines stress individualized shared decision-making, clinical trial participation, and coordination among surgical, radiation, medical oncologists, pathologists, and reconstructive surgeons [MS-1, MS-2]. For early-stage disease, locoregional control (BCS + RT or mastectomy ± PMRT) and systemic therapy (adjuvant or preoperative) aim to reduce recurrence risk. For metastatic disease, goals are palliation, prolongation of survival, and quality of life maintenance [BINV-21, BINV-28].

by_intent.patient_group: cT1–3, cN0 or N+, M0 invasive breast cancer and DCIS
by_intent.approach: Surgery (BCS or mastectomy) ± RT ± adjuvant systemic therapy based on receptor status, genomic risk, and nodal involvement. Preoperative systemic therapy preferred for HER2+ or TNBC ≥cT2 or ≥cN1, or for large primary relative to breast size [BINV-L 1].

by_intent.patient_group: Inoperable (IBC, cT4, cN2–3) or select operable (HER2+, TNBC, large tumor)
by_intent.approach: Systemic therapy (chemotherapy ± HER2-targeted therapy ± immunotherapy) followed by surgery and adjuvant therapy as indicated. Response guides subsequent locoregional and systemic decisions [BINV-L, BINV-14, BINV-15].

by_intent.patient_group: Stage IV (M1) or unresectable local/regional recurrence
by_intent.approach: Systemic therapy (endocrine ± targeted therapy, chemotherapy, ADCs, immunotherapy) with supportive care. Bone-modifying agents for bone metastases. Local therapy reserved for palliation or selected oligometastatic cases [BINV-21, BINV-28].

management_principles.mdt_requirements: Multidisciplinary team should include surgical oncologist, radiation oncologist, medical oncologist, pathologist, radiologist, reconstructive surgeon, genetic counselor, fertility specialist, and supportive care providers. Coordination is essential for sequencing of surgery, RT, and systemic therapy. Regular tumor board discussions are recommended [BINV-1, BINV-18, MS-2].
management_principles.performance_status_guide: Performance status is assessed using ECOG or Karnofsky scales. For adjuvant therapy, all patients with adequate PS are candidates. In metastatic setting, PS guides chemotherapy vs. endocrine-based therapy; patients with PS >2 may be better suited for endocrine therapy or supportive care alone. The PANEL recommends assessing PS at each reassessment to determine ongoing treatment value [BINV-M 1, BINV-Q 1, BINV-23, BINV-27].

management_pathways.title: Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (Tis, N0, M0)

nodes.criteria: Encapsulated or solid DCIS, Tis N0 M0

options.label: Breast-conserving surgery (BCS) without lymph node surgery
options.recommendation: BCS for DCIS; axillary staging not performed unless concern for invasion or anatomic limitation [DCIS-1]

options.label: Whole breast RT (WBRT) ± boost
options.recommendation: WBRT reduces IBTR by 50–70%; boost may be considered for higher risk [DCIS-1, footnote n]

options.label: Accelerated partial breast irradiation (APBI)
options.recommendation: For selected low-risk patients meeting ASTRO criteria [DCIS-1]

options.label: No RT
options.recommendation: Consider only for low-risk DCIS meeting RTOG 9804 criteria (screen-detected, grade 1–2, size ≤2.5 cm, margins ≥3 mm) [DCIS-1, footnote n]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy for 5 years considered for ER+ DCIS: tamoxifen for premenopausal, tamoxifen or AI for postmenopausal; low-dose tamoxifen if standard not tolerated. No survival advantage; risk reduction for ipsilateral and contralateral events [DCIS-2, MS-8].

options.label: Total mastectomy ± SLNB ± reconstruction
options.recommendation: SLNB strongly considered if mastectomy or if excision compromises future SLNB. Omission of SLNB may be considered in patients >50 years with low-grade ER+ DCIS [DCIS-1, footnote i].

options.label: Endocrine therapy for ER+ DCIS
options.recommendation: Tamoxifen (premenopausal) or tamoxifen/AI (postmenopausal) for 5 years; low-dose tamoxifen if symptomatic on 20 mg [DCIS-2]

management_pathways.title: Invasive Breast Cancer: HR-positive, HER2-negative, Postmenopausal, pT1–3, pN0 or pN+
branching_basis: Nodal status (pN0 vs. pN1–3 vs. pN2–3)

options.label: Consider adjuvant endocrine therapy (category 2B)
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy alone may be considered; chemotherapy not indicated [BINV-6]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy for risk reduction; no chemotherapy benefit based on low recurrence risk.

nodes.criteria: pT1b–T3 (>0.5 cm) and pN0 – not candidate for chemotherapy

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy alone ± ribociclib for eligible patients
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy alone; consider abemaciclib or ribociclib for high-risk based on clinicopathologic features [BINV-6, BINV-K 3 of 4]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy alone; no chemotherapy benefit if RS <26 (TAILORx). Consider CDK4/6 inhibitor for high-risk (≥4 LN or 1–3 LN with grade 3 or tumor ≥5 cm).

options.label: Strongly consider 21-gene RT-PCR assay (category 1)
options.recommendation: If RS ≥26, recommend adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy (category 1). If RS <26, endocrine therapy alone [BINV-6, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy benefit if RS ≥26; no benefit if RS <26 (postmenopausal). For premenopausal with RS 16–25, consider chemotherapy (unclear if benefit from suppression).

nodes.criteria: pN1 (1–3 positive nodes) – candidate for chemotherapy

options.label: Consider gene expression assay (e.g., 21-gene RS) for prognosis
options.recommendation: If RS ≥26, chemotherapy + endocrine therapy (category 1). If RS <26 in postmenopausal, endocrine alone; in premenopausal, consider chemotherapy for RS <26 as addition decreased distant recurrence but benefit unclear [BINV-6, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: For RS ≥26, adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy (category 1). For RS <26, postmenopausal no chemotherapy benefit; premenopausal may consider chemotherapy or OFS.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy (category 1)
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy indicated; consider abemaciclib or ribociclib for eligible patients; consider olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV [BINV-6, BINV-K 3 of 4, BINV-M]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± CDK4/6 inhibitor ± olaparib for high risk.

management_pathways.title: Invasive Breast Cancer: HR-positive, HER2-negative, Premenopausal, pT1–3, pN0
branching_basis: Ovarian function and desire for fertility

options.label: Consider adjuvant endocrine therapy (category 2B) ± ovarian suppression
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy alone; chemotherapy not indicated [BINV-7]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy for risk reduction; may include OFS depending on risk.

nodes.criteria: pT1b–T3 (>0.5 cm) and pN0 – not candidate for chemotherapy

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± OFS ± ribociclib for eligible patients
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy ± ovarian suppression; consider ribociclib for high-risk features [BINV-7, BINV-K 3 of 4]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy ± OFS; no chemotherapy if RS ≤15 or 16–25 if ovarian suppression considered.

nodes.criteria: pT1b–T3, pN0 – candidate for chemotherapy, RS ≤15

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± OFS
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy alone; no chemotherapy benefit (TAILORx) [BINV-7, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy ± OFS; no chemotherapy.

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± OFS
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy may offer small benefit but unclear if due to ovarian suppression; consider OFS + endocrine therapy as alternative [BINV-7, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Shared decision; chemotherapy or OFS may be considered.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± OFS
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy recommended (category 1) followed by endocrine therapy ± OFS [BINV-7, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy.

management_pathways.title: Invasive Breast Cancer: HR-positive, HER2-negative, Premenopausal, pT1–3, pN+

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± OFS
options.recommendation: If not candidate for chemotherapy: endocrine ± OFS; consider abemaciclib/ribociclib [BINV-8]

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine ± OFS
options.recommendation: Consider chemotherapy followed by endocrine ± OFS; consider gene expression assay [BINV-8]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy benefit depends on RS and menopausal status; for premenopausal, RS ≥26 clearly indicates chemotherapy; RS <26 may still benefit but ovarian suppression confounds.

nodes.criteria: pN1 (1–3 positive nodes) – candidate for chemotherapy

options.label: Consider gene expression assay to assess prognosis
options.recommendation: If RS ≥26: chemotherapy + endocrine ± OFS (category 1). If RS <26: endocrine ± OFS; consider chemotherapy for premenopausal as benefit seen in RxPONDER [BINV-8, BINV-N 2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: For RS ≥26, chemotherapy + endocrine. For RS <26 in premenopausal, chemotherapy may be considered as associated with lower distant recurrence; unclear if due to ovarian suppression.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine ± OFS (category 1)
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy + endocrine ± OFS; consider abemaciclib/ribociclib; consider olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV [BINV-8, BINV-M]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + endocrine ± OFS + targeted agents as indicated.

options.label: Consider adjuvant endocrine therapy alone
options.recommendation: Trastuzumab benefit uncertain in this population; endocrine therapy alone may be sufficient [BINV-5, footnote ii]

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy + trastuzumab + endocrine therapy (category 2B)
options.recommendation: Decision based on patient preference and toxicities [BINV-5]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy ± trastuzumab; absolute benefit negligible in very small tumors.

nodes.criteria: pT1b (0.6–1.0 cm) or pT1c–T3 (>1 cm) pN0/pN1mi

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy + trastuzumab + endocrine therapy
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy + HER2-targeted therapy (trastuzumab ± pertuzumab for pT2–T3) + endocrine therapy [BINV-5]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab ± pertuzumab for node-negative; endocrine therapy added.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy + pertuzumab + trastuzumab + endocrine therapy (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Preferred: chemotherapy + dual HER2 blockade + endocrine therapy. Based on APHINITY trial (11.3 yr follow-up confirms benefit of adding pertuzumab) [BINV-5, footnote mm]

options.label: Alternative: chemotherapy + trastuzumab + endocrine therapy (category 1)
options.recommendation: If pertuzumab not used, trastuzumab alone [BINV-5]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab ± pertuzumab + endocrine therapy. For high-risk, consider extended neratinib.

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy + trastuzumab (category 2B)
options.recommendation: Benefit uncertain due to lack of randomized trials in this subgroup [BINV-9, footnote ii]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Observation or endocrine therapy not applicable; consider trastuzumab with caution.

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy + trastuzumab
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab [BINV-9]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy + trastuzumab (category 1) ± pertuzumab for pT2–T3
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab; consider adding pertuzumab [BINV-9]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab ± pertuzumab.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy + pertuzumab + trastuzumab (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy + dual HER2 blockade preferred [BINV-9, footnote mm]

options.label: Alternative: chemotherapy + trastuzumab (category 1)
options.recommendation: If pertuzumab not used [BINV-9]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab ± pertuzumab.

options.label: No adjuvant therapy (category 2B: consider chemotherapy for high-risk features)
options.recommendation: Observation; chemotherapy may be considered for young patients with high-grade histology [BINV-10, footnote ss]

adjuvant_logic.summary: No routine adjuvant therapy.

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy ± adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy; olaparib for BRCA1/2 PV after completion of RT [BINV-10]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy ± olaparib for BRCA1/2 PV.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy (category 1) ± olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy; olaparib after RT [BINV-10]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy ± olaparif for BRCA1/2 PV.

options.label: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab (preop) → Cyclophosphamide/Doxorubicin + Pembrolizumab (preop) → Pembrolizumab (adjuvant) (category 1)
options.recommendation: KEYNOTE-522 regimen: preoperative chemo + pembro, adjuvant pembro [BINV-M 3]

adjuvant_logic.summary: After preoperative therapy, manage based on response: if ypT0N0, complete adjuvant pembro; if residual disease (ypT1–4,N0 or ypN≥1), consider adjuvant capecitabine and/or olaparib (if BRCA) ± pembro [BINV-16].

management_pathways.title: Favorable Histologies (Pure tubular, mucinous, cribriform, adenoid cystic, etc.)

nodes.criteria: ER-positive and/or PR-positive, HER2-negative, pure favorable histology, pN0 or pN1mi

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy for tumors ≥1 cm
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy alone generally sufficient; chemotherapy not indicated for pure favorable histologies [BINV-11]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy for ≥1 cm; no chemotherapy benefit unless atypical features.

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± adjuvant chemotherapy
options.recommendation: Endocrine therapy; consider chemotherapy if high-grade or other atypical features [BINV-11]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Endocrine therapy ± chemotherapy.

options.label: Local therapy only; systemic therapy only considered for pN+ disease
options.recommendation: Limited data; systemic therapy considered for node-positive [BINV-11]

management_pathways.title: Inflammatory Breast Cancer (IBC) – T4d, N0–N3, M0

options.label: Preoperative systemic therapy (anthracycline + taxane based ± trastuzumab ± pertuzumab if HER2+)
options.recommendation: Combined modality: preoperative chemo ± HER2-targeted therapy. For HER2+, pertuzumab-containing regimen may be administered [IBC-1, footnote i; IBC-2]

options.label: Total mastectomy + level I/II ALND (after response to preoperative therapy)
options.recommendation: Skin- and nipple-sparing mastectomy contraindicated. Delayed breast reconstruction [IBC-2]

options.label: RT to chest wall and comprehensive RNI
options.recommendation: After mastectomy, RT to chest wall + comprehensive RNI including undissected axilla [IBC-2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Complete planned chemo if not completed preoperatively; endocrine therapy if HR+; HER2-targeted therapy up to 1 year if HER2+ (category 1).

management_pathways.title: Paget Disease of the Nipple
branching_basis: Presence of associated breast cancer (DCIS or invasive) on imaging/biopsy

nodes.criteria: Examination or BI-RADS 4 or 5 imaging positive for breast lesion; core biopsy of lesion and NAC biopsy positive for Paget

options.label: Central lumpectomy including NAC ± SLNB + whole breast RT
options.recommendation: For patients with associated cancer; SLNB if invasive [PAGET-2]

options.label: Total mastectomy including NAC ± SLNB ± reconstruction
options.recommendation: Mastectomy always an option [PAGET-2]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Systemic adjuvant therapy as per stage and receptor status of underlying cancer. Endocrine therapy for ER+ DCIS or invasive cancer.

nodes.criteria: Examination and imaging negative for breast lesion; NAC biopsy positive for Paget

options.label: Consider breast MRI and tissue sampling if indicated
options.recommendation: MRI to identify occult lesion; if negative, clinical follow-up [PAGET-2]

management_pathways.title: Phyllodes Tumor (Benign, Borderline, Malignant)
branching_basis: Core biopsy result (benign, indeterminate, suspicious for borderline/malignant)

options.label: Excisional biopsy (complete mass removal, no margin intent)
options.recommendation: Excisional biopsy; no RT needed [PHYLL-1]

nodes.criteria: Core biopsy: borderline or malignant phyllodes

options.label: Wide local excision with ≥1 cm margins (lumpectomy or partial mastectomy)
options.recommendation: Total mastectomy only if negative margins cannot be achieved. No axillary staging unless clinically positive nodes [PHYLL-1, MS-90]

adjuvant_logic.summary: No adjuvant RT for positive margins; no proven role for endocrine or cytotoxic therapy. For systemic recurrence, see NCCN Soft Tissue Sarcoma.

options.label: Mastectomy + axillary staging (avoid BCS if RT delay >12–16 weeks)
options.recommendation: Mastectomy preferred; chemotherapy not in first trimester; begin adjuvant chemo in second trimester [PREG-1]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Adjuvant chemotherapy in second trimester; RT and endocrine therapy postpartum. Avoid trastuzumab, pertuzumab, ADCs, CDK4/6, PARP, ICIs [PREG-2].

options.label: Preoperative chemotherapy (anthracycline, cyclophosphamide, weekly paclitaxel) or proceed to surgery
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy can be given in second/third trimester; doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide preferred [PREG-1, PREG-2]

options.label: Mastectomy or BCS + axillary staging
options.recommendation: If BCS performed, RT delayed until postpartum [PREG-1]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Chemotherapy if indicated; RT and endocrine therapy postpartum.

options.label: Mastectomy or BCS + axillary staging
options.recommendation: Surgery after delivery; postpartum chemo/RT/endocrine as indicated [PREG-1]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Adjuvant therapy postpartum.

management_pathways.title: Recurrent/Stage IV (M1) Disease: HR-positive, HER2-negative
branching_basis: Prior endocrine therapy and disease-free interval

options.label: Cytotoxic therapy (chemotherapy or ADCs) first line
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy preferred for rapid disease control; may consider endocrine + targeted if not truly crisis [BINV-22, BINV-Q 2]

nodes.criteria: No visceral crisis, no prior endocrine therapy within 1 year

options.label: Endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor (preferred first-line)
options.recommendation: AI + ribociclib (category 1), AI + abemaciclib/palbociclib (2A). For premenopausal, add OFS [BINV-P 2, BINV-22]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Continue until progression/unacceptable toxicity; then switch.

nodes.criteria: No visceral crisis, prior endocrine therapy within 1 year or progression on adjuvant endocrine

options.label: Switch endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor or targeted therapy based on biomarkers
options.recommendation: Fulvestrant + CDK4/6i (if not previously used). For PIK3CA mutations: fulvestrant/inavolisib/palbociclib or alpelisib/fulvestrant. For ESR1: elacestrant, imlunestrant, vepdegestrant. For AKT1/PTEN: capivasertib/fulvestrant [BINV-P 2, BINV-Q 6]

adjuvant_logic.summary: After 3 endocrine regimens or symptomatic visceral, proceed to cytotoxic therapy.

management_pathways.title: Recurrent/Stage IV (M1) Disease: HER2-positive (HR-positive or negative)

options.label: Docetaxel + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab (THP) (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: With maintenance pertuzumab + trastuzumab; if HR+, add AI ± palbociclib [BINV-Q 4, BINV-24]

options.label: Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab (preferred)
options.recommendation: Alternative taxane schedule [BINV-Q 4]

options.label: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan + Pertuzumab (other recommended)
options.recommendation: Consider for select patients, e.g., with CNS involvement; risk of ILD [BINV-Q 4, footnote o]

options.label: Capecitabine/Tucatinib + Trastuzumab (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Especially for CNS progression; may be given in second line [BINV-Q 4, footnote p]

options.label: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: After prior pertuzumab and taxane; strong efficacy [BINV-Q 4]

options.label: T-DM1 (other recommended)
options.recommendation: If not candidate for fam-trastuzumab [BINV-Q 4, footnote r]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Continue HER2-targeted therapy until progression; then switch to other HER2 regimen.

management_pathways.title: Recurrent/Stage IV (M1) Disease: Triple-Negative Breast Cancer

options.label: Chemotherapy (albumin-bound paclitaxel, carboplatin/gemcitabine, or paclitaxel) + Pembrolizumab (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Pembrolizumab + chemo based on KEYNOTE-355 [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Sacituzumab govitecan + Pembrolizumab (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Based on ASCENT-04/KEYNOTE-D19 [BINV-Q 3]

nodes.criteria: First-line, PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor, and no germline BRCA1/2 PV

options.label: Sacituzumab govitecan (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Based on ASCENT-03 [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Datopotamab deruxtecan (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Based on TROPION-Breast02 [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Systemic chemotherapy
options.recommendation: Single agent sequential preferred [BINV-Q 5]

nodes.criteria: First-line, PD-L1 CPS <10 and germline BRCA1/2 PV

options.label: PARP inhibitor (olaparib or talazoparib) (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: PARPi more effective earlier [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Platinum (carboplatin or cisplatin) (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: As alternative [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Sacituzumab govitecan (category 1, preferred) if not used earlier
options.recommendation: Based on ASCENT [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan (other recommended) if HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-
options.recommendation: Based on DESTINY-Breast04 [BINV-Q 3]

options.label: Preoperative systemic therapy (chemo ± HER2-targeted ± immunotherapy)
options.recommendation: As per tumor subtype; see BINV-L, BINV-M [BINV-12, BINV-13]

adjuvant_logic.summary: After surgery, adjuvant systemic therapy based on residual disease and subtype. RT based on maximal stage at diagnosis and pathologic response.

nodes.criteria: Operable but preferred: HER2+ or TNBC ≥cT2 or ≥cN1; large tumor relative to breast; cN+ likely to convert

options.label: Preoperative systemic therapy (chemo ± HER2-targeted ± immunotherapy)
options.recommendation: As per tumor subtype [BINV-L, BINV-M]

adjuvant_logic.summary: Adjuvant therapy based on response: pCR → continue endocrine/HER2; residual disease → additional therapy (e.g., capecitabine for TNBC, T-DM1 or fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan for HER2+).

nodes.criteria: Operable but not candidate: extensive in situ disease, poorly defined tumor

options.label: Proceed to upfront surgery
options.recommendation: Preoperative therapy not recommended [BINV-L 1]

items.recommendation: Essential for all patients to assess extent of disease and comorbidities [BINV-1, DCIS-1]

items.text: Diagnostic bilateral mammogram; breast ultrasound as needed; axillary ultrasound if considering omission of surgical nodal staging
items.recommendation: Mammogram required; ultrasound for suspicious findings; axillary ultrasound for SLNB omission candidates >50 yrs with HR+/HER2-, grade 1–2, cT1N0 [BINV-1, footnote b]

May be used for staging extent of disease, especially for mammographically occult tumors, invasive lobular, axillary metastasis with occult primary; does not improve re-excision or survival rates [BINV-B, BINV-1, footnote c]

items.text: Chest diagnostic CT ± contrast; abdomen ± pelvis CT/MRI; bone scan or sodium fluoride PET/CT; FDG-PET/CT (as indicated)
items.recommendation: Only if symptoms or high risk for metastases; not routine for early-stage M0. For stage III, consider imaging [BINV-1, footnote i; BINV-12]
Mandatory for all invasive and noninvasive carcinomas; includes histologic type, grade, margin status [BINV-1, DCIS-1]
On all new primary and metastatic breast cancers. ER+: ≥1% staining; ER-low-positive: 1–10%; ER-: <1%. PR positive if ≥1% [BINV-A 4]

On all new primary and metastatic breast cancers. Define HER2 status per ASCO/CAP guidelines. IHC 0/0+/1+/2+/3+; reflex ISH for 2+. Distinguish HER2 null (IHC 0 absent), ultralow (0+), low (1+, 2+/ISH-) [BINV-A 1-3]
Consider if HR+ HER2- and considering adjuvant abemaciclib/ribociclib (Ki-67 ≥20% for ribociclib eligibility) [BINV-K 3, BINV-A]
For all patients ≤50, with TNBC at any age, with family history, or if candidate for PARP inhibitor. Also for males [BINV-1, footnote f; BINV-18]

items.text: Somatic multigene panel testing (tissue or ctDNA) for metastatic disease
items.recommendation: To identify candidates for targeted therapies: PIK3CA, AKT1, PTEN, ESR1, NTRK, MSI-H, TMB-H, RET, HER2 mutations [BINV-18, BINV-Q 6-8]

For HR+ HER2- early breast cancer to guide adjuvant chemotherapy and extended endocrine decisions. 21-gene RS preferred for pN0 (category 1) and pN1 (category 1 postmenopausal, category 2A premenopausal) [BINV-N]
All patients at diagnosis and during treatment. Address social determinants of health [BINV-1, DCIS-1, BINV-18]
Discuss impact of treatment on fertility; offer fertility preservation referrals for premenopausal patients desiring future pregnancy. Discuss birth control and breastfeeding [BINV-C]

items.text: Pregnancy test in all patients of childbearing potential
Genetic counseling if at risk for hereditary breast cancer
items.recommendation: Based on NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment guidelines [BINV-1]

surgery.role: Surgery is a cornerstone of locoregional treatment for breast cancer. For early-stage invasive cancer and DCIS, options are breast-conserving surgery (BCS) or mastectomy, both with equivalent survival outcomes when combined with appropriate adjuvant therapy. Axillary staging (sentinel lymph node biopsy or axillary lymph node dissection) guides systemic and radiation decisions. Preoperative systemic therapy can convert inoperable tumors to operable and increase BCS rates. For metastatic disease, role of primary tumor surgery is limited to palliation, with no proven survival benefit in prospective trials [MS-13–MS-18, BINV-2, BINV-3, BINV-14, BINV-15].
principles: The choice between BCS and mastectomy should be based on patient preference, tumor characteristics, ability to achieve negative margins, contraindications to RT, and genetic predisposition. BCS + RT offers equivalent survival to mastectomy (category 1) [BINV-2, BINV-3].

Negative margins are defined as 'no ink on tumor' for invasive cancer and DCIS component; for pure DCIS, ≥2 mm margin recommended. Re-excision should be attempted to achieve negative margins before converting to mastectomy [BINV-F].

Axillary staging: SLNB is preferred for clinically node-negative patients. ALND (level I/II) is indicated for biopsy-proven nodal metastases (without preoperative therapy) or residual disease after preoperative therapy. Omission of axillary staging may be considered in selected low-risk patients (e.g., >50 yrs, HR+/HER2-, grade 1–2, cT1N0) based on SOUND/INSEMA trials [BINV-D, BINV-E, footnote b].

For patients with clinically positive nodes converting to cN0 after preoperative systemic therapy, SLNB with targeted axillary dissection (TAD) of clipped node reduces false-negative rate. ALND still standard if SLN positive after preoperative therapy [BINV-D, BINV-14].

Oncoplastic techniques and reconstruction should be offered to patients undergoing mastectomy or BCS to optimize cosmetic outcomes. Skin-sparing and nipple-sparing mastectomy are options with careful patient selection [BINV-H].
For inflammatory breast cancer, mastectomy with level I/II ALND is recommended; skin-sparing and nipple-sparing are contraindicated. Delayed reconstruction is standard [IBC-2].

procedures.name: Sentinel Lymph Node Biopsy (SLNB)
procedures.approach: Peritumoral, subareolar, or subdermal injection of blue dye and/or radiotracer. Dual tracer improves identification.
procedures.indications: Clinically node-negative invasive breast cancer (cN0). For DCIS, consider only if mastectomy, concern for invasion, or oncoplastic BCS. For cN+ converting to cN0 after preoperative therapy, use with TAD [BINV-D, BINV-E].
procedures.key_complications: Lymphedema (lower risk than ALND), seroma, allergic reaction to dye, nerve injury (rare).

procedures.name: Axillary Lymph Node Dissection (ALND) – Level I/II
procedures.approach: Dissection of tissue inferior to axillary vein from latissimus dorsi to medial border of pectoralis minor. At least 10 nodes should be retrieved.
procedures.indications: Biopsy-proven axillary metastases (no preoperative therapy), residual disease after preoperative therapy, failed SLNB, or SLN-positive not meeting ACOSOG Z0011 criteria (e.g., mastectomy, >2 SLN+, T3, preoperative chemo). Also for clinically positive nodes [BINV-D, BINV-E].
procedures.key_complications: Lymphedema (15–30%), pain, numbness, restricted shoulder mobility, seroma, infection.

procedures.name: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS) / Lumpectomy / Partial Mastectomy
procedures.approach: Complete excision of tumor with a rim of normal tissue; orientation of specimen for margin assessment; clip placement for RT planning.
procedures.indications: All patients suitable for breast conservation: no contraindications (e.g., diffuse disease, multicentric with high-risk features, inability to receive RT, inflammatory cancer). For multicentric disease with specific criteria (age ≤40, TNBC, BRCA carrier, etc.), mastectomy may be mandated [BINV-2, BINV-G].
procedures.key_complications: Positive margins (10–20%), infection, hematoma, seroma, cosmetic deformity (reduced with oncoplastic techniques), fat necrosis.

procedures.name: Total Mastectomy
procedures.approach: Removal of all breast tissue, including nipple-areola complex (for nipple-sparing, NAC preserved). Skin-sparing or nipple-sparing variants allow immediate reconstruction.
procedures.indications: Patient choice, inability to obtain negative margins with BCS, contraindications to RT, inflammatory breast cancer, extensive disease, multicentric disease with high-risk features, homozygous ATM mutation (category 2B contraindication to BCS) [BINV-3, BINV-G].
procedures.key_complications: Infection, hematoma, seroma, flap necrosis, lymphedema (if axillary surgery), chest wall numbness, need for reconstruction.

procedures.name: Targeted Axillary Dissection (TAD)
procedures.approach: Preoperative clip placement in biopsied positive node; at surgery, injection of dual tracer, removal of SLNs and clipped node. Improves false-negative rate.
procedures.indications: Clinically node-positive patients who convert to cN0 after preoperative systemic therapy and desire SLNB alone instead of ALND [BINV-D, footnote k].
procedures.key_complications: Similar to SLNB; potential for missing residual nodal disease if not all clipped nodes retrieved.

surgery.resectability_criteria: Tumors are considered operable if they can be removed completely with negative margins without unacceptable morbidity. Inoperable tumors include those with extensive skin/chest wall involvement (T4a–c), inflammatory breast cancer (T4d), bulky or matted N2 nodes, or N3 disease. Preoperative systemic therapy can render these operable. Resectability for liver/lung oligometastases is considered on a case-by-case basis in multidisciplinary setting; for de novo oligometastatic disease with favorable features, aggressive multimodality approach may be considered [BINV-15, BINV-18].

radiation_therapy.role: Radiation therapy is an integral component of breast cancer management, used for local control after BCS (whole breast RT or APBI), after mastectomy (PMRT) in high-risk patients, and for regional nodal irradiation (RNI) in node-positive disease. RT also has a role in palliation of metastatic sites. Hypofractionation is the preferred schedule for whole breast and chest wall RT. RT sequencing with systemic therapy generally follows chemotherapy; concurrent use with CMF is acceptable but not with capecitabine or olaparib [BINV-I, MS-18–MS-25].
principles: Individualize RT planning using 3D CT-based planning with target volume delineation and organs at risk (heart, lung) constraints. Use DIBH, prone positioning, cardiac blocks to reduce cardiac dose [BINV-I 1].

Hypofractionated WBRT (40–42.5 Gy/15–16 fractions) is preferred; conventional fractionation (45–50.4 Gy/25–28 fractions) may be used in selected cases. Boost to tumor bed (10–16 Gy/4–8 fx) for higher-risk patients [BINV-I 1].
Ultra-hypofractionation (28.5 Gy/5 weekly fractions or 26 Gy/5 daily fractions) may be considered for selected low-risk patients; optimal boost technique not established [BINV-I 1].

PMRT indicated for: 1–3 positive nodes (strongly consider) and ≥4 positive nodes (category 1). Consider for node-negative with high-risk features (T3, central/medial, grade 3, ER-negative, young age, LVI). For patients with positive margins after neoadjuvant therapy, if re-excision not feasible, strongly consider PMRT [BINV-I 2, BINV-3].

RNI includes supraclavicular, infraclavicular, internal mammary, and axillary nodes at risk. Internal mammary coverage individually based on tumor location and nodal involvement. Dose: 46–50 Gy conventional or 39–42.56 Gy hypofractionated [BINV-I 2].

For patients receiving preoperative systemic therapy, RT decisions based on maximal stage (initial clinical stage + pathologic response). For cN1 ypN0, RNI not routinely indicated (NSABP B-51 data); for cN2–3 ypN0 or any ypN+, comprehensive RNI recommended [BINV-14, BINV-I 2].

dose_frameworks.name: Conventional WBRT
dose_frameworks.indication: Selected cases when hypofractionation not suitable (e.g., large breast, reconstruction) [BINV-I 1]

dose_frameworks.name: Moderate Hypofractionation (WBRT or Chest Wall)
dose_frameworks.indication: Preferred for whole breast RT; also used for chest wall and RNI [BINV-I 1, BINV-I 2]

dose_frameworks.name: Ultra-Hypofractionation (WBRT)
dose_frameworks.schedule: 26 Gy daily; 28.5 Gy once weekly for 5 weeks
dose_frameworks.indication: Select patients >50 yrs with early-stage, node-negative disease; boost not established [BINV-I 1]

dose_frameworks.name: Boost to Tumor Bed
dose_frameworks.schedule: Sequential after WBRT or as simultaneous integrated boost (48 Gy/15 fx with WBRT 40 Gy/15 fx)
dose_frameworks.indication: Higher risk (age <50, high grade, positive margins, DCIS with boost benefit) [BINV-I 1]

dose_frameworks.name: APBI (External Beam)
dose_frameworks.indication: Preferred APBI regimen for patients meeting ASTRO 2024 criteria: ≥40 yrs, ER+, ≤2 cm, grade 1–2, negative margins, node-negative [BINV-I 3]

dose_frameworks.name: APBI (Brachytherapy)
dose_frameworks.total_dose: 34 Gy/10 fx BID, 32 Gy/8 fx BID, or 30.1 Gy/7 fx BID
dose_frameworks.indication: Same patient selection as external beam APBI [BINV-I 3]

approaches.name: Whole Breast RT (WBRT) after BCS
approaches.dose_fractionation: 40–42.5 Gy/15–16 fx preferred; 45–50.4 Gy/25–28 fx optional; boost 10–16 Gy/4–8 fx
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: CMF can be given concurrently; capecitabine after RT; olaparib after RT; endocrine therapy can be concurrent or sequential [BINV-I 2]
approaches.indication: After BCS for invasive cancer and DCIS; category 1 for node-positive, category 2A for node-negative. Omission may be considered for low-risk patients ≥70 yrs with ER+ T1N0 (category 1) or ≥65 yrs with T ≤3 cm and endocrine therapy [BINV-2]
approaches.key_trial: EBCTCG meta-analysis: WBRT reduces 10-yr recurrence and 15-yr breast cancer mortality (RR 0.52, 0.82 respectively) [MS-21].
approaches.toxicities: Skin reaction, fatigue, breast edema, fibrosis, telangiectasia; cardiac (left-sided); lung (pneumonitis) – reduced with DIBH and hypofractionation.

approaches.name: Post-Mastectomy RT (PMRT)
approaches.dose_fractionation: 45–50.4 Gy/25–28 fx or 40–42.5 Gy/15–16 fx; scar boost 10 Gy/4–5 fx if high risk; bolus for skin involvement
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: None (capecitabine, olaparib after RT); endocrine and HER2 therapy may be concurrent [BINV-I 2]
approaches.indication: All node-positive patients (category 1 for ≥4 nodes; strongly consider for 1–3 nodes). Consider for node-negative with T3, positive margins, or high-risk features. For IBC: mandatory [BINV-3, IBC-2]
approaches.key_trial: EBCTCG meta-analysis: PMRT reduces recurrence and breast cancer mortality in node-positive patients [MS-23].
approaches.toxicities: Chest wall skin changes, fibrosis; cardiac, lung; lymphedema; impaired reconstruction cosmesis (higher with implant).

approaches.name: Regional Nodal Irradiation (RNI)
approaches.dose_fractionation: 46–50 Gy/25–28 fx or 39–42.56 Gy/15–16 fx; boost to grossly involved nodes
approaches.indication: For node-positive disease: ≥4 positive nodes (category 1); 1–3 nodes: strongly consider, especially if central/medial tumors, T3, or other risk factors. Also for cN2–3 ypN0 or any ypN+ after preoperative therapy [BINV-2, BINV-14, BINV-I 2]
approaches.key_trial: MA.20: improved DFS (82% vs 77%) and reduced distant recurrence with RNI. EORTC 22922: improved breast cancer mortality at 15 years [MS-20, MS-21].
approaches.toxicities: Increased cardiac dose (especially internal mammary); lung; lymphedema; potential brachial plexopathy (rare).

approaches.name: Accelerated Partial Breast Irradiation (APBI)
approaches.dose_fractionation: 30 Gy/5 fx QOD (preferred) or 40 Gy/15 fx; brachytherapy options
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: Can be administered prior to chemotherapy if needed [BINV-2, footnote v]
approaches.indication: For patients without germline BRCA1/2 PV meeting ASTRO 2024 criteria: ≥40 yrs, ER+ invasive DC ≤2 cm, grade 1–2, negative margins, no LVSI, node-negative. Also for DCIS ≤2 cm, low- to intermediate-grade, negative margins [BINV-I 3]
approaches.key_trial: Florence trial (APBI vs WBRT): comparable local control; better cosmesis and less toxicity with APBI. NSABP B-39: 10-yr IBTR 4.6% vs 3.9% (not equivalent but small absolute difference) [MS-21].
approaches.toxicities: Lower acute toxicity; fair/poor cosmesis in some studies (especially 3D conformal); fat necrosis, breast pain.

systemic_therapy.overview: Systemic therapy for breast cancer includes endocrine therapy, chemotherapy, HER2-targeted therapy, immunotherapy (checkpoint inhibitors), antibody-drug conjugates, PARP inhibitors, and other targeted therapies. Treatment decisions are based on tumor subtype (HR/HER2), stage, genomic profiles, menopausal status, germline mutations, and patient fitness. For early-stage disease, adjuvant systemic therapy reduces recurrence and mortality. Preoperative systemic therapy is preferred for high-risk subtypes (TNBC, HER2+) and for downstaging. For metastatic disease, sequential therapy is used with goals of prolonging survival and maintaining quality of life. The NCCN Guidelines provide comprehensive regimens with dosing schedules and toxicity management [BINV-M, BINV-Q, MS-30–MS-86].

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Endocrine therapy alone (tamoxifen or AI) ± OFS
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: TAILORx: RS <26 no chemotherapy benefit in postmenopausal; premenopausal RS ≤15 no benefit, RS 16–25 may benefit from chemotherapy or OFS [BINV-6, BINV-7, BINV-N 2]
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Abemaciclib or ribociclib for high-risk (≥4 LN or 1–3 LN with grade 3 or tumor ≥5 cm)

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Chemotherapy → endocrine therapy ± abemaciclib/ribociclib; ± olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Chemotherapy benefit established for RS ≥26 (RxPONDER, SWOG 8814). Abemaciclib (monarchE) reduces risk of recurrence in high-risk. Olaparib (OlympiA) improves OS in BRCA1/2 [BINV-5, BINV-7, BINV-M]

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab + pertuzumab (category 1, preferred for node-positive) ± endocrine therapy
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: APHINITY: pertuzumab benefit confirmed at 11.3 yr follow-up. Trastuzumab standard of care [BINV-5, BINV-9, BINV-M 4]
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Chemotherapy + trastuzumab alone (category 1). For residual disease after preoperative: T-DM1 or fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan (category 1)

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: For stage II–III: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab → AC + Pembrolizumab → adjuvant pembrolizumab (category 1)
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: For stage I: TC or dose-dense AC→T. For residual disease: capecitabine and/or olaparib if BRCA. For BRCA: olaparib (OlympiA) [BINV-10, BINV-16]

by_stage_and_setting.setting: Metastatic – HR+ HER2- (first-line, no visceral crisis)
by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Endocrine therapy (AI) + CDK4/6 inhibitor (ribociclib category 1; abemaciclib or palbociclib category 2A)
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Ribociclib + AI improves OS (MONALEESA-2, -7). For early progression on adjuvant endocrine: fulvestrant + CDK4/6i or targeted therapy based on PIK3CA, AKT1, PTEN, ESR1 [BINV-P 2, BINV-22]
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Fulvestrant alone, AI alone, tamoxifen. For visceral crisis: chemotherapy (single agent sequential). For PIK3CA: inavolisib triplet or alpelisib. For ESR1: elacestrant, imlunestrant, vepdegestrant

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Docetaxel + Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab (THP) (category 1) followed by maintenance pertuzumab + trastuzumab; if HR+, add AI ± palbociclib
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: CLEOPATRA: improved PFS and OS with pertuzumab. PATINA: palbociclib improves PFS in maintenance [BINV-Q 4, MS-73]
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Paclitaxel + pertuzumab + trastuzumab (preferred). Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan + pertuzumab (other recommended)

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Chemotherapy + pembrolizumab OR sacituzumab govitecan + pembrolizumab (both category 1)
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: KEYNOTE-355: pembrolizumab + chemo improves OS in CPS ≥10. ASCENT-04: SG + pembro improves PFS [BINV-Q 3]
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: SG alone, datopotamab deruxtecan, PARP inhibitor (if BRCA), platinum (if BRCA)
